text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT: Margaret Chen
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: 1977
SEX: Female
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: Hematology/Oncology Service
ACCESSION NUMBER: HP-[REDACTED]
PROCEDURE DATE: [TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
REPORT DATE: [TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: [TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION
-----------------------
Specimen A: Bone marrow core biopsy, right posterior iliac crest
Specimen B: Bone marrow aspirate, right posterior iliac crest (smears, clot section, and touch preparations)
Specimen C: Peripheral blood smear (submitted concurrently for morphologic review)

CLINICAL HISTORY
----------------
This is a 48-year-old woman with no prior hematologic malignancy who presents with a 3-week history of progressive fatigue, easy bruising, gingival bleeding, and unintentional weight loss. Peripheral blood CBC at presentation: WBC 87,400/uL with circulating blasts (~40%), hemoglobin 6.8 g/dL, platelets 32,000/uL. Clinical examination notable for gingival hyperplasia with spontaneous bleeding and splenomegaly. Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate submitted for evaluation of suspected acute leukemia. Concurrent peripheral blood smear submitted for morphologic review. Flow cytometry immunophenotyping performed on aspirate material. Cytogenetic and molecular studies submitted from same aspirate specimen; results to follow under separate report(s).

TYPE OF PROCEDURE
-----------------
Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate (bilateral posterior iliac crest approach; right side utilized as primary); peripheral blood smear review.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Specimen A — Core Biopsy:
Received in 10% neutral buffered formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number. The specimen consists of a tan-pink core of bone marrow tissue measuring 2.2 cm in length x 0.3 cm in diameter. The core is intact without fragmentation. Submitted in its entirety for decalcification and sectioning. Three representative H&E-stained sections prepared at multiple levels. Reticulin and PAS stains performed.

Specimen B — Aspirate:
Received as multiple direct smears (6 slides), touch impregnation preparations (4 slides), and aspirate material submitted in EDTA tube for clot section preparation and flow cytometry. Clot section prepared after formalin fixation. Smears stained with Wright-Giemsa. Estimated specimen adequacy: adequate for evaluation. Spicules present on 3 of 6 smear slides. A 500-cell manual differential performed on the optimal smear.

Specimen C — Peripheral Blood Smear:
Received as two Wright-Giemsa-stained peripheral blood smear slides, labeled with patient's name and MRN. Smears well-prepared; both examined.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

CORE BIOPSY (Specimen A):
The core biopsy is markedly hypercellular, with estimated cellularity of 90-95% (virtually entirely replaced by hematopoietic cells; fat spaces nearly absent). The marrow space is diffusely infiltrated by a monotonous population of large, immature cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, vesicular nuclei with one to three prominent nucleoli, and scant to moderate amounts of pale basophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are readily identified. Scattered Auer rod-containing cells are appreciated on the touch preparations and confirmed on aspirate smears (see below). Residual erythropoiesis and thrombopoiesis are markedly diminished. Scattered mature neutrophils are present but represent a minor fraction of the total cellularity. The bony trabeculae show no osteoblastic or osteoclastic activity and no fibrosis beyond trace reticulin (MF-0 by European Consensus grading). Sinusoidal architecture is effaced by the neoplastic infiltrate. No granulomas, necrosis, or metastatic carcinoma identified.

ASPIRATE SMEARS AND CLOT SECTION (Specimen B):
The aspirate smears are markedly hypercellular with abundant spicules. The 500-cell differential reveals:
  - Myeloblasts: 72%
  - Promyelocytes: 3%
  - Myelocytes/metamyelocytes: 2%
  - Segmented neutrophils and bands: 5%
  - Monocytes: 2%
  - Erythroid precursors: 10%
  - Lymphocytes: 5%
  - Plasma cells: 1%

The blast population consists of large cells with round to slightly irregular nuclei, finely dispersed (open) chromatin, prominent nucleoli (one to three per cell), and moderate amounts of pale to lightly basophilic cytoplasm. Azurophilic granules are present in the cytoplasm of a subset of blasts. Auer rods — elongated, needle-like, magenta-staining cytoplasmic inclusions — are identified in approximately 8-12% of blasts on optimal smear preparation; no faggot cells (bundles of Auer rods) are observed. These findings are pathognomonic of myeloid differentiation and exclude lymphoblastic leukemia. The clot section confirms the findings from the core biopsy with sheets of immature blasts. Erythroid and megakaryocytic elements are markedly reduced. Megakaryocytes present are morphologically normal without dysplastic features.

PERIPHERAL BLOOD SMEAR (Specimen C):
The peripheral smear confirms leukocytosis with a marked left shift. Circulating blasts account for approximately 38-42% of the differential count. The blasts morphologically resemble those described in the aspirate, with round nuclei, open chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and moderate basophilic cytoplasm; Auer rods are identified in a subset of circulating blasts. Significant neutropenia with relative predominance of immature granulocytic forms (blasts, occasional promyelocytes). Anemia with normochromic, normocytic red blood cells and no dysplastic erythroid changes (no megaloblastic changes, no ring sideroblasts anticipated). Thrombocytopenia confirmed. No evidence of microangiopathic hemolytic anemia (no schistocytes). No leukoerythroblastic picture beyond the leukemic involvement.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A. FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (Performed on bone marrow aspirate, 8-color multiparameter panel):

Gating strategy: Blast population gated on CD45-dim/low side-scatter population, comprising approximately 71% of total events analyzed (consistent with blast percentage on morphologic differential). Results are reported for the blast population.

Blast Population Immunophenotype:
  CD45:        Dim positive (characteristic of myeloblast gating)
  CD34:        Positive (bright, 88% of blast gate)
  CD117 (KIT): Positive (86% of blast gate)
  CD33:        Positive (moderate intensity, 91% of blast gate)
  CD13:        Positive (78% of blast gate)
  MPO:         Positive (intracytoplasmic, 74% of blast gate)
  HLA-DR:      Positive (82% of blast gate)
  CD7:         Dim positive (aberrant, 42% of blast gate — see comment)
  CD64:        Negative (<5%)
  CD14:        Negative (<5%)
  CD11b:       Negative (<5%)
  CD56:        Negative (<5%)
  CD19:        Negative (<5%)
  CD10:        Negative (<5%)
  CD3 (surface and cytoplasmic): Negative
  CD2:         Negative (<5%)
  TdT:         Negative (<5%)
  CD20:        Negative (<5%)
  Glycophorin A: Negative on blast gate
  CD41/CD61:   Negative on blast gate

Comment on CD7 aberrancy: Dim CD7 expression is present on approximately 42% of the blast gate. CD7 is a T-cell associated antigen and its expression on AML blasts is a well-described aberrancy occurring in approximately 10-20% of AML cases; it does not indicate T-lymphoid lineage commitment in this context, particularly given strong co-expression of myeloid-associated markers (CD33, CD13, MPO, CD117) and complete absence of other T-cell markers (CD2, CD3, CD5). This pattern is consistent with myeloid lineage with aberrant CD7 expression.

Flow Cytometry Interpretation: The immunophenotypic profile of the blast population is consistent with ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA, with aberrant CD7 expression. The blast population meets criteria for myeloid lineage per WHO/ICC 2022 criteria (MPO positivity and myeloid marker expression). No evidence of mixed phenotype acute leukemia (MPAL). No monocytic differentiation markers. No megakaryoblastic or erythroid differentiation markers.

B. SPECIAL STAINS:
  - Myeloperoxidase (MPO) histochemical stain on aspirate smear: POSITIVE in blast population (granular cytoplasmic positivity in >50% of blasts, consistent with myeloid lineage)
  - Sudan Black B (SBB): POSITIVE (parallel distribution to MPO)
  - Periodic Acid-Schiff (PAS): Variable weak cytoplasmic positivity; no block positivity (arguing against pure erythroid leukemia)
  - Non-specific esterase (NSE/alpha-naphthyl acetate): NEGATIVE to weakly positive; pattern not consistent with monocytic differentiation
  - Iron stain (Prussian Blue): Reduced marrow iron stores; no ring sideroblasts identified in the erythroid precursors
  - Reticulin stain: MF-0; no significant reticulin fibrosis

C. CYTOGENETICS / KARYOTYPE (Conventional G-banding, Bone Marrow Aspirate):
Result: 46,XX [20/20 metaphases analyzed]
Interpretation: Normal female karyotype. All 20 metaphases analyzed demonstrated a normal 46,XX chromosomal complement with no structural or numerical abnormalities identified. No evidence of t(8;21)(q22;q22.1), inv(16)(p13.1q22), t(15;17)(q22;q12), or other recurrent AML-associated cytogenetic abnormalities.

Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH) Panel — AML-Directed:
  t(8;21) / RUNX1-RUNX1T1:   NEGATIVE
  inv(16) or t(16;16) / CBFB-MYH11: NEGATIVE
  t(15;17) / PML-RARA:       NEGATIVE
  MLL (KMT2A) rearrangement (11q23): NEGATIVE
  del(5q):                   NEGATIVE
  del(7q) / monosomy 7:      NEGATIVE
  del(17p) / TP53 locus:     NEGATIVE
  +8 (trisomy 8):            NEGATIVE

FISH Interpretation: No recurrent AML-associated chromosomal rearrangements or copy number abnormalities detected by the AML FISH panel. Results are consistent with normal karyotype AML (NK-AML).

D. MOLECULAR STUDIES — STATUS: PENDING
The following molecular studies have been submitted from this same bone marrow aspirate specimen and results will be reported under a SEPARATE MOLECULAR PATHOLOGY / NGS REPORT upon completion:
  - FLT3-ITD and FLT3-TKD (D835) by fragment analysis and PCR
  - NPM1 mutation analysis (exon 12)
  - CEBPA mutation analysis (bZIP domain, biallelic assessment)
  - IDH1 (R132) and IDH2 (R140, R172) mutation analysis
  - Comprehensive myeloid next-generation sequencing (NGS) panel
  - Additional mutation testing as clinically indicated

Results of molecular studies are critical for ELN 2022 risk stratification and treatment planning and will be communicated to the treating team immediately upon completion. Estimated turnaround time: 7-10 business days.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSIS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A. BONE MARROW CORE BIOPSY, RIGHT POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST:
   ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA (AML), NOT OTHERWISE SPECIFIED (NOS)
   - Markedly hypercellular bone marrow (estimated cellularity 90-95%)
   - Blasts comprise 72% of total nucleated cells by 500-cell aspirate differential
   - Auer rods present; myeloid lineage confirmed by histochemistry and immunophenotype
   - Normal female karyotype (46,XX) by conventional cytogenetics [20/20 metaphases]
   - No recurrent AML-associated cytogenetic abnormalities by FISH
   - Aberrant CD7 expression noted (clinically relevant for MRD monitoring)
   - Molecular studies PENDING (see above)
   - Final classification deferred pending molecular results; preliminary WHO/ICC 2022 designation: AML, NOS

B. BONE MARROW ASPIRATE, PERIPHERAL BLOOD SMEAR, CLOT SECTION:
   Consistent with diagnosis above (see detailed findings in Microscopic Description)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COMMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The morphologic, cytochemical, and immunophenotypic findings are diagnostic of acute myeloid leukemia. The blast percentage of 72% significantly exceeds the WHO threshold of ≥20% for AML diagnosis. The presence of Auer rods is pathognomonic for myeloid differentiation and effectively excludes ALL/LBL. The blast immunophenotype — CD34+, CD117+, CD33+, CD13+, MPO+ (flow and histochemistry), HLA-DR+, with aberrant dim CD7 — is entirely consistent with AML with no morphologic or immunophenotypic features to suggest a specific recurrent genetic entity (pending molecular data). The absence of t(8;21), inv(16), and t(15;17) by FISH, and the normal karyotype by G-banding, establish this as cytogenetically normal AML (NK-AML). No significant dysplastic changes are appreciated in the residual non-blast hematopoietic elements; combined with the de novo clinical presentation (no prior MDS or antecedent hematologic disorder documented), the findings do not support a classification of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes on morphologic grounds alone.

Final ELN 2022 risk stratification is deferred pending molecular studies, particularly FLT3-ITD allelic ratio, NPM1, CEBPA, and comprehensive NGS panel results. These results will be critical for determining the prognostic category and guiding decisions regarding allogeneic stem cell transplantation in first remission.

This case was reviewed at the hematopathology sign-out and discussed with the treating hematology/oncology team.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SIGNED:
Attending Hematopathologist
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Electronic signature on file]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
